source_jobs.title: Head of Client Experience
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity

Project Expedition is looking for a hands-on, AI-forward Head of Client Experience to help us scale an organization already known for exceptional service, without losing the personal touch our clients, travel advisors, partners, and travelers expect.

This is not a strategy-only CX leadership role. We are looking for someone who leads from the front: someone who will learn the product deeply, understand our workflows, spend time in the queue, handle tickets when needed, take ownership of escalations, engage directly with customers, and coach the team from firsthand experience.

Our belief is simple: in travel, service cannot feel automated. Travel is emotional, time-sensitive, and often high-stakes. The right leader will know how to use AI to make our team faster, smarter, more consistent, and more proactive, while preserving the judgment, empathy, and human care that define an exceptional client experience.

You will own the day-to-day performance of the Client Experience team, the systems that support it, and the service philosophy behind it. Your mandate is to turn support into a measurable competitive advantage through operational excellence, product mastery, thoughtful AI adoption, and hands-on leadership.

 

What You’ll Own

You will lead our Client Experience function across email, chat, and phone, managing team leads and support specialists while staying close enough to the work to understand what is really happening on the front lines.



You will be expected to:

 - Lead, coach, and develop team leads and support specialists across email, chat, and phone.

 - Spend regular time in the ticket queue and reporting to understand customer pain points, product gaps, process friction, and team execution.

 - Personally handle escalations, difficult customer situations, VIP/advisor issues, and time-sensitive travel problems when needed.

 - Learn and master the Project Expedition product, booking flows, supplier/operator workflows, advisor needs, internal tools, and customer journey.

 - Build a culture where leaders model the standard, not just measure it.

 - Own key operating metrics, including CSAT, SLEs, QA scores and other service-quality indicators.

 - Establish and improve QA programs, calibration routines, coaching cadences, SOPs, escalation paths, and training materials.

 - Improve workforce planning, scheduling, coverage models, training ramps, and peak-season readiness.

 - Use data to identify the root causes of customer friction and turn those insights into clear operational, product, or tooling improvements.

 - Partner closely with Product, Engineering, Sales, and Supplier teams to improve support tooling, internal workflows, and the customer experience.

 - Bring clear recommendations to leadership on what is working, what is broken, what needs investment, and where we can scale more effectively.

AI + Human Touch

We are not looking for someone who wants to “automate customer service” at the expense of quality. We are looking for someone who understands how to combine AI, automation, better systems, and human judgment to deliver a higher level of service than either people or technology could deliver alone.



You should be excited to explore and implement practical AI use cases such as:

 - AI-assisted ticket triage, routing, prioritization, and summarization.

 - Agent-assist tools that help the team respond faster while preserving warmth, accuracy, and brand voice.

 - Better knowledge management and internal search so the team can answer confidently, accurately, and consistently.

 - QA support, trend detection, sentiment analysis, and coaching insights.

 - Automation of repetitive administrative work so humans can focus on complex, emotional, urgent, or high-value interactions.

 - Proactive alerts and workflows for time-sensitive travel issues, supplier changes, cancellations, itinerary questions, and customer risk signals.



You should also know where not to automate. The right

source_jobs.title: Senior Travel Planning Specialist for Custom & Group Tours
source_jobs.description:
Project Expedition, a fast-growing travel startup, is looking for a Travel Planning Specialist to join our Custom and Groups team. This role focuses on creating custom itineraries and quotes for travel advisors and their clients. If you enjoy travel, problem solving, and finding the right solution for complex requests, you’ll fit right in.

As part of the Custom and Groups team, you’ll design, quote, sell, and support custom experiences for travelers around the world. You’ll work on everything from one-off private tours to large leisure or corporate group programs.

Key Responsibilities

 - Communicate with travel advisors through email and phone using our internal ticketing system

 - Deliver prompt, friendly, and professional service

 - Work with tour operators around the world to build custom and group itineraries

 - Create tailored proposals and quotes based on each group’s needs

 - Manage requests ranging from single custom tours to multi day group programs

 - Troubleshoot issues in real time when needed

 - Suggest creative ideas and unique experiences to enhance trips

 - Build and maintain tour content, pricing, and logistics for group excursions and custom programs

 - Coordinate capacities, schedules, and operational details with vendors

 - Keep advisors and operators updated on itinerary or schedule changes

 - Support advisors and group leaders with questions and bookings

 - Monitor penalty schedules, cost confirmations, agreements, and deadlines

 - Review guest feedback and share insights to improve future programs

The ideal candidate:

 - Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, power point.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Ability to multi-task, prioritize and problem solve in a fast past environment.

 - Ability to manage changing priorities and meet deadlines

 - Strong interpersonal and customer service skills.

 - Strong analytical capabilities with a high level of attention to detail

 - Works well independently and as part of a team

 - Is fluent in written and spoken English (Other languages desirable)

Benefits include:

 - Annual company bonus (up to a total of $3,000, based on company targets - deferred eligibility)

 - 15 days of leave (increases to 20 days at 2 years tenure)

 - Company Healthcare Plan (Company contribution $9K with 2 completely covered plans)

 - 401K company plan

 - Annual work from home budget $500

 - Annual training budget of $250

 - TSA Precheck (worth $80)

 - Annual Project Expedition tour credits of $750

 - Employee discounts on thousands of tours and activities

Additional Information

While we’re excited to grow our team across the U.S., we’re currently unable to hire employees residing in California or New York

Please note that while this is a remote position, it is not suitable for digital nomads. Employees must be based in the United States and will have an allocation of time that they can work remotely in other destinations.

Project Expedition is growing very rapidly, and we expect that this role will evolve over time. As a result, you will not only have the chance to make a direct impact on a growing company, but you will have the opportunity to shape and influence your career path. This position is great for someone who thrives without a lot of structure, works independently, responds well to change and a fluid working environment, and who always tries to find a better way of doing things.

source_jobs.title: Supplier Operations Associate
source_jobs.description:
Supplier Operations Associate

Project Expedition, a rapidly growing travel company, is seeking a Supplier Operations Associate to join our Supplier Partnerships Team. This role is focused on managing day-to-day supplier operations, maintaining accurate product and pricing information, and ensuring our Tour Operator network runs smoothly. If you enjoy solving problems, working with data, building supplier relationships, and improving operational processes, this could be a great fit.

The right candidate is highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable managing multiple priorities at once. You will work closely with Tour Operators and internal teams to resolve issues, maintain product quality, and support the ongoing growth of our marketplace. This is a hands-on role that requires strong communication skills, operational thinking, and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

Key Responsibilities:

Manage product and pricing updates across the platform

 - Maintain accurate pricing, availability, and product information across systems

 - Coordinate product profile updates, including activations and deactivations

 - Oversee content updates such as inclusions, meeting points, and product descriptions

 - Identify and escalate data inconsistencies to the appropriate internal teams

Support new product launches and removals

 - Coordinate with internal stakeholders to launch new products

 - Review product content, pricing, and availability before products go live

 - Assess and manage product removals when operational issues arise

Investigate and resolve pricing and operational issues

 - Research pricing discrepancies using rate sheets, booking records, and internal systems

 - Determine whether issues are operational or technical in nature

 - Manage complex supplier issues through resolution while keeping stakeholders informed

 - Document recurring issues and supplier-specific requirements to improve efficiency

Build and maintain supplier relationships

 - Conduct regular meetings with Tour Operators to discuss performance, commissions, and growth opportunities

 - Respond to supplier inquiries and operational requests

 - Identify opportunities to strengthen supplier partnerships and improve collaboration

Manage supplier account maintenance

 - Maintain CRM records and supplier contact information

 - Support commission updates, rebranding initiatives, and account structure changes

 - Ensure supplier data remains accurate across platforms

 - Prepare operational reports and performance updates for leadership

Support compliance and reconciliation efforts

 - Track and follow up on supplier insurance documentation requirements

 - Partner with internal teams to review compliance processes and identify improvements

 - Investigate payment disputes and reconcile discrepancies using historical pricing and booking information

Leverage AI to optimize and automate repetitive tasks and processes

The ideal candidate:

 - 2-4 years of experience in operations, account management, supplier support, or a similar role

 - Strong organizational skills and exceptional attention to detail

 - Comfortable managing large volumes of data across multiple systems and platforms

 - Strong problem-solving and analytical abilities

 - Clear and effective communicator with both internal and external stakeholders

 - Able to work independently and prioritize competing responsibilities

 - Experience working with external partners, vendors, or suppliers

 - Familiarity with APIs and how pricing, availability, and content flow between systems is a plus

 - Marketplace, e-commerce, or travel industry experience is preferred

 - Fluent in English with strong written communication skills; additional languages are a bonus

Benefits include:

 - Annual company bonus (up to a total of $3,000, based on company targets - deferred eligibility)

 - 15 days of leave (increases to 20 days at 2 years tenure)

 - Company Healthcare Plan (Company contribu

source_jobs.title: Content Development Associate
source_jobs.description:
Supply-Content Specialist

Project Expedition, a rapidly growing travel company, is seeking a Supply-Content Specialist to join our Supplier Partnerships Team. This role is focused on onboarding new Tour Operators and suppliers, ensuring they are successfully launched and positioned for long-term success on our platform. If you enjoy building relationships, managing complex projects, working cross-functionally, and helping partners grow, this could be a great fit.

The right candidate is highly organized, detail-oriented, and comfortable managing multiple onboarding projects simultaneously. You will serve as the primary point of contact for suppliers throughout the onboarding process, helping them navigate content requirements, technical setup, and platform best practices. This is a hands-on role that combines relationship management, content quality, operational execution, and problem-solving.

Key Responsibilities

Support supplier onboarding from initial engagement through activation

 - Manage the onboarding process for assigned suppliers, ensuring a smooth and efficient launch experience

 - Follow established onboarding processes, playbooks, and documentation to maintain consistency and quality

 - Track and achieve onboarding KPIs, including time to launch, activation rates, and content quality standards

 - Coordinate onboarding activities across multiple internal teams

Build and maintain supplier relationships

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for suppliers throughout onboarding and early-stage development

 - Provide guidance, training, and best-practice recommendations to help suppliers succeed on the platform

 - Respond to supplier questions and proactively address onboarding challenges

 - Escalate complex issues when necessary while maintaining a positive supplier experience

Manage content onboarding and quality assurance

 - Review supplier content to ensure it meets platform standards and quality expectations

 - Optimize product descriptions, inclusions, meeting points, imagery, and other content for conversion

 - Conduct quality assurance reviews before products go live

 - Partner with suppliers to resolve content gaps and improve overall product quality

Support technical onboarding and integrations

 - Work closely with Supply Operations and technical teams to support supplier integrations

 - Assist with onboarding-related technical tasks and connectivity validation

 - Troubleshoot day-to-day onboarding issues and identify whether they are operational or technical in nature

 - Collaborate with internal teams to ensure issues are resolved efficiently

Contribute to process improvement and reporting

 - Identify onboarding bottlenecks and opportunities to improve efficiency

 - Recommend updates to onboarding workflows, documentation, and playbooks

 - Track supplier onboarding performance and share insights with the broader team

 - Support initiatives that improve supplier activation, content quality, and long-term supplier success

Leverage AI to optimize and automate repetitive tasks and processes

The ideal candidate:

 - 2-4 years of experience in supplier onboarding, account management, operations, content management, or a similar role

 - Strong organizational and project management skills

 - Exceptional attention to detail and commitment to quality

 - Comfortable managing multiple onboarding projects simultaneously

 - Strong communication and relationship-building skills

 - Ability to work cross-functionally with both operational and technical teams

 - Basic understanding of how APIs deliver pricing, availability, and content into a platform

 - Comfortable working with large amounts of data across multiple systems and CRMs

 - Self-motivated and able to work independently in a fast-paced environment

 - Marketplace, e-commerce, or travel industry experience is preferred

 - Fluent in English with strong written communication skills

Benefits include:

 - Annual company bonus (up

source_jobs.title: Content Expansion Team Lead
source_jobs.description:
Project Expedition, a rapidly growing travel company, is seeking a Team Leader for our Content Development Team. As part of our Supplier Partnerships Department, this role is focused on leading supplier onboarding and content quality, ensuring new tour operators are set up for success on our platform from day one. If you enjoy building processes, working cross-functionally, and helping partners succeed, this could be a great fit.

The right candidate will lead by example, while onboarding new suppliers, managing early-stage relationships, and ensuring high-quality content and seamless integrations. You’ll play a key role in scaling our supply inventory and improving how we bring new experiences onto the platform. This is a hybrid role where you’ll lead a small team while also staying hands-on as an individual contributor.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead and mentor a team focused on onboarding new suppliers

 - Own the onboarding process from initial engagement through activation

 - Serve as the main point of contact for suppliers during onboarding

 - Partner with technical teams to support integrations and resolve issues

 - Ensure all supplier content meets quality standards and is optimized for conversion

 - Improve onboarding workflows, documentation, and playbooks

 - Track and drive performance against onboarding KPIs (time to launch, activation, quality)

 - Identify bottlenecks and continuously improve processes

 - Collaborate with product, operations, and commercial teams to share insights and improve the platform

 - Analyze supplier and onboarding data to drive better outcomes

 - Support early-stage supplier success through guidance and best practices

 - Leverage AI to optimize and automate repetitive tasks and processes


THE IDEAL CANDIDATE:

 - Experience leading or mentoring a team

 - Strong organizational and project management skills

 - Clear and effective communicator

 - Comfortable working cross-functionally, including with technical teams

 - Detail-oriented with strong analytical thinking

 - Able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

 - Strong relationship-building skills

 - Experience working with external partners or vendors

 - Marketplace, onboarding, or travel industry experience is a plus


BENEFITS INCLUDE:

 - Annual company bonus (up to a total of $3,000, based on company targets - deferred eligibility)

 - 15 days of leave (increases to 20 days at 2 years tenure)

 - Company Healthcare Plan (Company contribution $9K with 2 completely covered plans)

 - 401K company plan

 - Annual work from home budget $500

 - Annual training budget of $250

 - TSA Precheck (worth $80)

 - Annual Project Expedition tour credits of $750

 - Employee discounts on thousands of tours and activities


ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

While we’re excited to grow our team across the U.S., we’re currently unable to hire employees residing in California or New York.

Please note that while this is a remote position, it is not suitable for digital nomads. Employees must be based in the United States and will have an allocation of time that they can work remotely in other destinations.

Project Expedition is growing quickly, and this role will likely evolve over time. You’ll have the opportunity to make a direct impact on how we scale our supply and onboarding efforts, while also shaping your own career path. This role is great for someone who is comfortable with ambiguity, works independently, and is always looking for ways to improve how things are done.

notes: Travel company with operational roles, no explicit tech roles
